summary: Quote review call for the new operations management proposal. Rachel had reviewed the quote on her phone twice but the system was showing 'Offline' — David noted this is a recurring issue that's making him hesitant to send proposals without immediate follow-up. Rachel had specific questions about the implementation timeline and integration with their existing ERP.

transcript.speaker: rep
transcript.name: David
transcript.text: Rachel, thanks for jumping on. I sent the proposal Monday — wanted to walk through any questions.

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.name: Rachel
transcript.text: Yes, I read it Sunday night and again on the train Tuesday morning.

transcript.speaker: rep
transcript.name: David
transcript.text: Interesting — my system showed 'Offline' status the whole time. That's been happening more lately.

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.name: Rachel
transcript.text: Strange. Maybe phone reading doesn't register?

transcript.speaker: rep
transcript.name: David
transcript.text: Honestly I'm not sure what the threshold is. I've started just calling instead of waiting on the activity panel — I can't tell if 'Offline' means 'never looked' or 'looked yesterday and the system didn't catch it.'

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.name: Rachel
transcript.text: That's actually useful feedback for your product team. Anyway, my main question is the implementation timeline.

transcript.speaker: rep
transcript.name: David
transcript.text: Sure. We're estimating 6-8 weeks for the standard rollout, but with the Oracle integration you mentioned, probably 10.

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.name: Rachel
transcript.text: Can the Oracle piece run in parallel with the rest, or is it sequential?

transcript.speaker: rep
transcript.name: David
transcript.text: Parallel for the most part. I can have our solutions architect detail the dependencies.

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.name: Rachel
transcript.text: Please. I'd like to share that with our IT director.
